data.title: Add cross-runtime support via harness adapter architecture
data.description:
DSO is currently Claude Code-only. Add a cross-runtime compatibility layer so DSO skills, hooks, and workflows can function across multiple AI coding tool runtimes.

## Reference: everything-claude-code Cross-Harness Adapter

everything-claude-code (131k stars) achieves cross-runtime parity across 4 runtimes via an adapter.js that normalizes each harness's stdin event format into a shared format, then routes to shared scripts/hooks/*.js:

- **Claude Code** (8 hook events)
- **Cursor** (20 hook events)
- **OpenCode** (20+ hook events)
- **Codex**

This DRY architecture means adding a new hook behavior touches one file regardless of how many runtimes are supported. An AGENTS.md file at the repo root serves as the universal cross-tool routing file read by all harnesses.

## Scope for DSO

1. **Adapter layer** — create a runtime adapter that normalizes event formats from different AI coding tools into DSO's internal event model
2. **AGENTS.md generation** — add AGENTS.md generation to /dso:onboarding alongside CLAUDE.md, providing a universal entry point for non-Claude runtimes
3. **Skill portability** — evaluate which DSO skills can work cross-runtime (planning, review) vs. which are Claude-specific (sub-agent dispatch via Agent tool)
4. **Hook portability** — identify which hooks map to equivalent events in other runtimes (pre-commit hooks are git-level and already portable; PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks are Claude-specific)
5. **Graceful degradation** — when running on a less-capable runtime, DSO should degrade gracefully (e.g., skip sub-agent dispatch, fall back to single-agent workflows) rather than fail

## Design Considerations

- The agentskills.io open standard (SKILL.md format) is already portable across Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI — DSO skills already use this format
- Git-level hooks (pre-commit, pre-push) work regardless of runtime
- Claude Code-specific features (Agent tool, model selection, hook events) need abstraction or graceful fallback
- Cross-runtime testing strategy needed — how do we validate behavior on Cursor/Codex/OpenCode?

Reference: https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code
